title: Verify lockfile integrity hashes on install
description: Install from a committed lockfile with integrity hashes enforced so a tampered or substituted package fails the build instead of installing silently.
body: Use when installing dependencies in CI or a reproducible build. Do not run an install that ignores or regenerates the lockfile, and avoid disabling integrity checks to make an install pass. Actions: use the frozen/locked install mode (npm ci, pnpm --frozen-lockfile, pip --require-hashes, cargo --locked), confirm the lockfile carries integrity hashes, and inspect that a hash mismatch aborts the install; record the resolved versions. Validation signals include a frozen install that fails on any hash or version drift and an unchanged lockfile after install. Risk boundary: a hash mismatch means the package content differs from what was reviewed; treat it as potential tampering, deny the install, and escalate rather than regenerating the lockfile to move past it.
